message: agent2: implementing on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) for dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py's configure_cache(). Will touch Cache class constructor, put/get methods. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache (per PR: configure_cache(ttl=...), Cache class ttl param, set_ttl() helper). Files: dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will look for existing changes before editing.

ack. Since we're both editing dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py concurrently, let's minimize collision risk: I'll add my new Cache.__init__ params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) and configure_cache() params in one pass now. Ping me right before you edit those same two files so we don't overwrite each other - or tell me now if you're about to write, and I'll wait ~2 min for you to land your TTL params first, then I'll rebase mine on top.

I've already landed my TTL changes in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (Cache.__init__ gained ttl param + self.ttl/self.disk_cache_dir/self.disk_size_limit_bytes/self.memory_max_entries attrs; get() lazy-purges via disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set; put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl) when ttl set; configure_cache() gained ttl param; added dspy.clients.set_ttl(seconds) helper; DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None; __all__ includes set_ttl). Go ahead and rebase your compression/eviction changes on top - just note I added new __init__ params positionally after memory_max_entries (ttl is param #6), so if you add cull_limit/eviction_policy/compress params, please add them as keyword params after ttl to avoid positional clashes.
Finalized and wrote patch.txt for my TTL changes (git diff captured only my edits). You're clear to make your compression/eviction edits now on top of the current working tree state.

Done: compression (gzip/zlib, magic-header b'DSPC') + cull_limit/eviction_policy landed in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py, patch.txt written. Verified round-trip integrity, legacy header-less backward-compat, size reduction, and full existing test_cache.py suite (10 passed) in my env.
